_comment: Operational coverage/depth guards for pre-activation review. These are not expert competency scores.

source_coverage._rationale: Expected explicit source counts come from the source-level curriculum fast-track discovered in ra_knowledge. This is a coverage guard: each RA owner should have the known jurisdiction-specific source set seeded before automation review.

self_doc_depth_floors._rationale: Self-doc counts are only a memory-volume proxy. They are useful as a pre-activation floor, but they do not prove expert competence.

ra_us.basis: Post-recovery US corpus has materially deeper memory volume; keep automation from running if this mature baseline collapses.

ra_eu.basis: Post-recovery EU corpus has enough MDR/IVDR memory depth for baseline automation review.

ra_kr.basis: #59 manual KR growth target; minimum viable KR memory depth before considering automation review.

relative_depth_floors.basis: Temporary #60 guard to prevent KR from remaining near-empty compared with EU after asymmetric bootstrap/recovery. This is not a regulatory expertise benchmark and must not be presented as expert maturity.

expert_maturity._rationale: Expert maturity must be judged from behavior and feedback, not self-doc volume alone.
